1.1.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of my spiritual master. I offer my respectful obeisances to Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who appeared in this world with Nityananda Prabhu and is surrounded by His devotees.

1.2.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the two lotus feet Srimati Radharani. I offer my respectful obeisances Lord Nandanandana, who is surrounded by the gopis and enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

1.3.verse_text: The celebrated personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana are briefly but truthfully described with great delight in both Vedic literatures and oral tradition. This book will also describe them.

1.4-5.verse_text: These associates of the Lord are described by the residents of Mathura-mandala, by the various books written by devotees, various Vedic literatures such as the Puranas and Agamas and by the great devotees and saintly persons. For the satisfaction of my dear friends, these associates of the Lord will now be briefly describing in writing, following the previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God.

1.6.verse_text: The associates of Krsna who reside in Vrajabhumi may be divided into three groups : 1. protectors of domestic animals, 2. brahmanas, 3. others.

questions.question: Into how many distinct categories are the residents of Vrajabhumi who serve as Krsna's associates divided?
questions.explanation: The residents of Vrajabhumi are classified into exactly three groups: protectors of domestic animals, brahmanas, and others.

questions.question: Which of the following is listed as one of the three primary groups of Krsna's associates residing in Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The associates of Krsna are categorized into three groups, the first of which is the protectors of domestic animals.

questions.question: Which specific group of residents in Vrajabhumi is categorized alongside protectors of domestic animals and others in the classification of Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: Brahmanas are explicitly identified as the second of the three groups of Krsna's associates in Vrajabhumi.

1.7.verse_text: The Protectors of Domestic Animals: These are descendants of the Yadu dynasty and they may be divided into three groups : 1. vaisyas, 2. abhiras, 3. gurjaras.

questions.question: The descendants of the Yadu dynasty who act as protectors of domestic animals are categorized into which three specific groups?
options: Gopas, Gopis, and Gopas of the forest
questions.explanation: The Yadu dynasty descendants who protect animals are divided into the three groups of Vaisyas, Abhiras, and Gurjaras.

questions.question: Which family line characterizes the protectors of domestic animals within the Vraja community?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that those who protect domestic animals are descendants of the Yadu dynasty.

1.8.verse_text: The Vaisyas: For the most part the most important vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows. Some of the vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and they are known as abhiras.

questions.question: What is the primary occupation of the most important Vaisyas in the context of Vraja?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the most important Vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows.

questions.question: How are the Abhiras described in terms of their lineage?
options: They are born from lower-caste mothers
They are born from brahmana mothers
They are born from ksatriya mothers
They are born from kshatriya fathers and vaisya mothers
questions.correct_answer: They are born from lower-caste mothers
questions.explanation: The verse states that some of the Vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and are known as Abhiras.

1.9.verse_text: The Abhiras: The abhiras are those who have vaisya fathers and sudra mothers. A little inferior to the vaisyas, who are engaged in cow protection, the abhiras earn their livelihood by protecting buffalo. They are known by various names, such as ghosa.

questions.question: What is the specific lineage of the abhiras regarding their parents?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the abhiras as being born of vaisya fathers and sudra mothers.

questions.question: Which animal are the abhiras primarily responsible for protecting as their livelihood?
questions.explanation: The text states that the abhiras distinguish themselves from the cow-protecting vaisyas by earning their livelihood through the protection of buffalo.

questions.question: What is an alternative name used for the abhiras?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that the abhiras are known by various names, specifically mentioning ghosa.

questions.question: How is the social status of the abhiras described in relation to the vaisyas who protect cows?
options: A little inferior to the vaisyas
questions.correct_answer: A little inferior to the vaisyas
questions.explanation: The text clearly states the abhiras are a little inferior to the vaisyas who are engaged in cow protection.

1.10.verse_text: The Garjaras: A little inferior to the abhiras, the garjaras earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals. Their bodily features are a little chubby and they reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

questions.question: What primary occupation defines the livelihood of the Garjaras living in Vraja?
questions.explanation: The Garjaras are described as earning their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals.

questions.question: How are the bodily features of the Garjaras typically described?
questions.explanation: The scriptural description explicitly mentions that the bodily features of the Garjaras are a little chubby.

questions.question: In which part of the Vraja region do the Garjaras reside?
options: In the center of the village
Near the banks of the Yamuna
questions.explanation: The Garjaras are noted for residing on the outskirts of Vraja.

1.11.verse_text: The Brahmanas: Learned in all the Vedas, the brahmanas engage in performing sacrifices, worshiping the Deity and other brahminical occupations.

questions.question: What is the primary scriptural expertise of the brahmanas residing in Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The brahmanas in Vraja are described as being learned in all the Vedas.

questions.question: Which specific activities do the brahmanas of Vraja perform as part of their daily occupations?
options: Performing sacrifices and worshiping the Deity
Trading in dairy products and ghee
Tending to the cows and calves
questions.correct_answer: Performing sacrifices and worshiping the Deity
questions.explanation: The brahmanas are described as engaging in the performance of sacrifices and the worship of the Deity.

1.12.verse_text: The Bahisthas: The bahisthas earn their livelihood by engaging in various trades and crafts. In this way we have described five kinds of associates (vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas and bahisthas) of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: What is the primary means of livelihood for the class of associates in Vrajabhumi known as the Bahisthas?
options: Engaging in various trades and crafts
Cultivating the fields and grain crops
questions.correct_answer: Engaging in various trades and crafts
questions.explanation: The Bahisthas are defined by their occupation of engaging in various trades and crafts to earn their livelihood.

questions.question: How many categories of Lord Hari's associates in Vrajabhumi are systematically enumerated in the list that includes Vaisyas, Abhiras, Garjaras, Brahmanas, and Bahisthas?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly categorizes the associates of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi into five distinct groups: Vaisyas, Abhiras, Garjaras, Brahmanas, and Bahisthas.

1.13.verse_text: Lord Krsna's associates may also be divided into the following eight groups : 1. worshipable superiors, 2. relatives who are on the same level (such as brothers and sisters) 3. gopi messengers, 4. servants, 5. craftsmen, 6. maidservants, 7. contemporary cowherd friends and 8. dear gopi friends. Included among the worshipable superiors are the brothers and similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja, Nanda's contemporary friends, servants, other associates and the elderly gopis. Krsna is always respectful to these superiors.

questions.question: Which specific group of Lord Krsna's associates is categorized as the first of the eight groups in the classification of His associates?
questions.explanation: The classification lists worshipable superiors as the first of the eight groups of Krsna's associates.

questions.question: Besides brothers and sisters, which category of Krsna's associates comprises relatives who are considered to be on the same level?
questions.explanation: The second group consists of relatives on the same level, such as brothers and sisters.

questions.question: Who are included within the category of Krsna's worshipable superiors alongside the elderly gopis and Nanda Maharaja's contemporary friends?
options: Brothers, similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja, servants, and other associates
Gopi messengers and contemporary cowherd friends
questions.correct_answer: Brothers, similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja, servants, and other associates
questions.explanation: Worshipable superiors are described as including Nanda's brothers, similar relatives, contemporary friends, servants, other associates, and elderly gopis.

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly listed as the seventh group of Lord Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: The list defines the seventh group as contemporary cowherd friends.

questions.question: Which group constitutes the eighth and final category of Krsna's associates listed in this classification?
questions.explanation: Dear gopi friends are listed as the eighth group of Krsna's associates.

1.14.verse_text: 1. Worshipable Superiors: The worshipable superiors include Krnsa's grandfather, other elderly relatives and also the community of brahmanas.

questions.question: Which specific group of individuals, besides elderly relatives and the grandfather, are categorized as the worshipable superiors of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the worshipable superiors include Krsna's grandfather, other elderly relatives, and the community of brahmanas.

questions.question: Who among the following is explicitly listed as a worshipable superior of Lord Krsna within the classification of superiors?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the grandfather, along with other elderly relatives and the community of brahmanas, as the worshipable superiors of Krsna.

1.15-16.verse_text: Krsna's fair-complexion, white-haired and white-attired grandfather is named Parjanya Maharaja, because he is like a great cloud (parjanya) showering the nectar of auspiciousness. He is the best of all the residents of Vraja. Desiring a worthy descendant, he followed the advice of Devarsi Narada and engaged in the worship of Lord Narayana, the husband of Laksmi. As he was worshiping Narayana at Nandisvara-pura, a pleasant voice spoke the following words from the sky.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Krsna's grandfather, Parjanya Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja is described as having a fair complexion.

questions.question: What is the hair color of Krsna's grandfather, Parjanya Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja is described as having white hair.

questions.question: Which color of attire is traditionally worn by Krsna's grandfather, Parjanya Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja is specifically described as being white-attired.

questions.question: Which celestial personality instructed Parjanya Maharaja to worship Lord Narayana?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja followed the advice of Devarsi Narada to perform the worship of Lord Narayana.

questions.question: At which specific location was Parjanya Maharaja engaged in the worship of Lord Narayana?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja performed his worship of Lord Narayana at Nandisvara-pura.

questions.question: What is the reason provided for the name of Krsna's grandfather, Parjanya Maharaja?
options: He is like a roaring lion of righteousness
He is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
He is the foundation of the Vraja community
He is the elder of the cowherd kings
questions.correct_answer: He is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
questions.explanation: The name Parjanya is used because he is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness.

1.17-18.verse_text: "Because of your pious austerities you will obtain five sons. Of these sons the middle one, named Nanda, will be the best. Nanda's glorious son will delight the residents of Vraja. He will defeat all oppositions. Both the demigods and demons will worship Him, touching the jewels of their crowns to His lotus feet."

questions.question: How many sons was Parjanya Maharaja destined to obtain as a result of his pious austerities?
questions.explanation: The divine voice from the sky foretold that Parjanya Maharaja would obtain five sons.

questions.question: Which of Parjanya Maharaja's sons was identified by the divine voice as being the best among them?
questions.explanation: The divine voice explicitly stated that among the five sons, the middle one named Nanda would be the best.

questions.question: Whose glorious son was predicted to bring delight to all the residents of Vraja?
questions.explanation: The divine prophecy declared that Nanda's glorious son would delight the residents of Vraja.

1.19.verse_text: Hearing this, Parjanya Maharaja became happy and decided to reside at the place. He lived there until he saw the Kesi demon arrive and at that time he became frightened and fled with all his associates to Mahavana.

1.20.verse_text: Krsna's grandmother Variyasi is respected in the land of Vraja. She is short in stature and her complexion is the colour of a kusumbha flower. She is attired in green garments and her hair is the color of milk.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: Variyasi, Krsna's grandmother, is specifically described as having the complexion of a kusumbha flower.

questions.question: Which specific shade of garments is Krsna's grandmother Variyasi described as wearing?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Variyasi is attired in green garments.

questions.question: What is the color of the hair of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: Variyasi's hair is described as being the color of milk.

questions.question: How is the physical stature of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi, characterized?
questions.explanation: Variyasi is described as being short in stature.

1.21.verse_text: Parjanya Maharaja's brothers are named Urjanya and Rajanya and his sister is an expert dancer named Suverjana. Her husband is named Gunavira and they live in the town named Surya-kunja.

questions.question: What are the names of Parjanya Maharaja's two brothers?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja has two brothers named Urjanya and Rajanya.

questions.question: What is the name of Parjanya Maharaja's sister who is celebrated as an expert dancer?
questions.explanation: Suverjana is identified as the sister of Parjanya Maharaja and is described as an expert dancer.

questions.question: Who is the husband of Suverjana?
questions.explanation: Suverjana is the wife of Gunavira.

questions.question: In which town do Suverjana and her husband Gunavira reside?
questions.explanation: The residence of Suverjana and Gunavira is the town of Surya-kunja.

1.22-23.verse_text: Krsna's father is Nanda Maharaja. Nanda delights the residents of Vraja and he is worshiped by all the worlds. He has a protuberant belly, his complexion is the colour of sandalwood, he is tall in stature and his garments are the colour of a bandhujiva flower. His beard is a mixture of black and white hairs, like rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Nanda Maharaja?
options: The color of a bandhujiva flower
The color of a dark rain cloud
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja is explicitly described as having a complexion the color of sandalwood.

questions.question: Which specific flower color is used to describe the garments worn by Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja's garments are described as being the color of a bandhujiva flower.

questions.question: What is the specific physical appearance of Nanda Maharaja's beard?
options: A mixture of black and white hairs resembling rice and toasted sesame seeds
Reddish like the rays of the rising sun
questions.correct_answer: A mixture of black and white hairs resembling rice and toasted sesame seeds
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja's beard is described as a mixture of black and white hairs, specifically compared to rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together.

questions.question: Which of the following physical characteristics is attributed to Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text states that Nanda Maharaja has a protuberant belly.

1.24.verse_text: Nanda is the king of the cowherd men, the younger brother of Upananda and the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva. Nanda and his wife Yasoda are the king and queen of Vrajabhumi and the parents of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: Who is the elder brother of the king of the cowherd men, Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja is specifically described as the younger brother of Upananda.

questions.question: Which personality is recognized as the intimate friend of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja is explicitly identified as the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva.

questions.question: Along with Nanda Maharaja, who serves as the queen of Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The text states that Nanda and his wife Yasoda are the king and queen of Vrajabhumi.

1.25.verse_text: Because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu), Nanda Maharaja is also known as Vasudeva. He is also famous by the name Anakadundubhi and it is known that in his previous birth he was named Drona.

questions.question: Which specific name is attributed to Nanda Maharaja due to his possession of great wealth?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja is known as Vasudeva because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu).

questions.question: By what other prominent name is Nanda Maharaja famous, as identified in the description of his titles?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nanda Maharaja is also famous by the name Anakadundubhi.

questions.question: What was the name of Nanda Maharaja in his previous birth?
questions.explanation: It is recorded that in his previous birth, Nanda Maharaja was known as Drona.

1.26.verse_text: These names of Maharaja Nanda are described in the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana. Nanda's most intimate friend is named Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as the most intimate friend of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Maharaja Vrsabhanu is the most intimate friend of Maharaja Nanda.

1.27.verse_text: Krnsa's mother is named Yasoda because she bestows (da) fame (yasah) on the cowherd residents of Vraja. She is like the personification of parental love for Krnsa. Her complexion is dark and her garments are like a rainbow.

questions.question: For what reason is the mother of Krsna famously addressed as Yasoda?
options: She is the daughter of the sun god
She bestows fame on the cowherd residents of Vraja
She was born on the banks of the Yamuna
She possesses the greatest beauty in all of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: She bestows fame on the cowherd residents of Vraja
questions.explanation: The name Yasoda is derived from her quality of bestowing (da) fame (yasah) upon the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: Which emotion is Yasoda considered to be the personification of in relation to Krsna?
questions.explanation: Yasoda is described as being like the personification of parental love for Krsna.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Krsna's mother, Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The description specifically states that Yasoda's complexion is dark.

questions.question: What is the appearance of the garments worn by Yasoda?
questions.explanation: Yasoda's garments are described as being colorful like a rainbow.

1.28.verse_text: Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, neither large nor small. She has long black hair. Her closest friends are Aindavi and Kirtida.

questions.question: What is the physical build of Mother Yasoda?
options: She is very tall and thin
She is of a medium size, neither large nor small
She is very short and stout
questions.correct_answer: She is of a medium size, neither large nor small
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, neither large nor small.

questions.question: How is the hair of Mother Yasoda described in terms of length and color?
questions.explanation: The text describes Mother Yasoda as having long black hair.

questions.question: Who are the two closest friends of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Aindavi and Kirtida as the closest friends of Mother Yasoda.

1.29.verse_text: Yasoda is the wife of Nanda, the king of Gokula. She is the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja, the friend of Devaki and the mother of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: Who is recognized as the husband of Mother Yasoda and the king of Gokula?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Yasoda as the wife of Nanda, the king of Gokula.

questions.question: Mother Yasoda holds the queenly position over which specific community in Vraja?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Yasoda as the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as the dear friend of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly states that Yasoda is the friend of Devaki.

questions.question: Who is the son of Mother Yasoda mentioned in the description of her relations?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names Yasoda as the mother of Lord Krsna.

1.30.verse_text: The friendship of Yasoda is described in the following statement of the Adi Purana : "The wife of Maharaja Nanda was known by two names, Yasoda and Devaki. Partly because they share the same name (Devaki) the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva were great friends."

questions.question: What are the two names by which the wife of Maharaja Nanda is known?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the wife of Maharaja Nanda was known by the two names, Yasoda and Devaki.

questions.question: What is cited as a reason for the deep friendship between the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva?
options: They share the same name of Devaki
They are both residents of Mathura
They share the same birth star
questions.correct_answer: They share the same name of Devaki
questions.explanation: The verse explains that part of the reason for their great friendship is that they share the same name, Devaki.

1.31.verse_text: Lord Balarama's mother is named Rohini, because she is filled with a constantly rising (arohini) flood of transcendental bliss. Although she dearly loves her son Balarama, she loves Krsna millions of times more.

questions.question: What is the primary reason given for the name of Lord Balarama's mother, Rohini?
options: She was born in the land of Rohini-kunda
She is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
She serves the Divine Couple with constant devotion
She is the daughter of a king named Rohina
questions.correct_answer: She is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The name Rohini is explained by her being filled with a constantly rising (arohini) flood of transcendental bliss.

questions.question: How does the intensity of Mother Rohini's love for Lord Krsna compare to her love for her own son, Balarama?
options: She loves Balarama slightly more than Krsna
She loves them both exactly the same
She loves Krsna millions of times more than Balarama
She loves Balarama exclusively as her only son
questions.correct_answer: She loves Krsna millions of times more than Balarama
questions.explanation: Though she loves her son Balarama, her affection for Krsna is described as being millions of times greater.

1.32.verse_text: Nanda Maharaja's elder brothers are Upananda and Abhinanda and his younger brothers are Sannanda and Nandana. These are the names of Lord Krsna's parental uncles.

questions.question: Who are the two elder brothers of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja's elder brothers are clearly identified as Upananda and Abhinanda.

questions.question: Who are the two younger brothers of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja's younger brothers are identified as Sannanda and Nandana.

questions.question: What is the collective relationship of Upananda, Abhinanda, Sannanda, and Nandana to Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Upananda, Abhinanda, Sannanda, and Nandana specifically as the parental uncles of Lord Krsna.

1.33.verse_text: Upananda's complexion is pink. He has a long beard and wears green garments. Tungi-devi is his dear wife. Both her complexion and garments are the colour of a cataka bird.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Nanda Maharaja's elder brother, Upananda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Upananda's complexion is pink.

questions.question: What color are the garments worn by Upananda?
questions.explanation: Upananda is described as wearing green garments.

questions.question: What is the name of the wife of Upananda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Tungi-devi as the dear wife of Upananda.

questions.question: What color is both the complexion and the garments of Tungi-devi?
options: The color of a cataka bird
The color of a bandhujiva flower
The color of a blue lotus
questions.correct_answer: The color of a cataka bird
questions.explanation: Tungi-devi's complexion and garments are both described as being the color of a cataka bird.

1.34.verse_text: Abhinanda wears dark garments and his long beautiful beard is like a great conchshell. Pivari-devi is his wife. She wears blue garments and her complexion is pink.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Abhinanda?
questions.explanation: Abhinanda is described as wearing dark-colored garments.

questions.question: To what object is the long beard of Abhinanda compared?
questions.explanation: Abhinanda's long and beautiful beard is described as being like a great conchshell.

questions.question: Who is the wife of Abhinanda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Pivari-devi as the wife of Abhinanda.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Pivari-devi?
questions.explanation: Pivari-devi is described as having a pink complexion.

questions.question: What color are the garments worn by Pivari-devi?
questions.explanation: Pivari-devi is described as wearing blue garments.

1.35.verse_text: Sannanda is also known as Sunanda. His complexion is white and he wears dark-colored clothing. He has two or three white hairs and he is very dear to Lord Kesava.

questions.question: By what other name is the cowherd Sannanda known?
questions.explanation: Sannanda is explicitly stated to be also known as Sunanda.

questions.question: What is the complexion of the cowherd Sannanda?
questions.explanation: Sannanda's complexion is described as white.

questions.question: What color garments does Sannanda wear?
questions.explanation: Sannanda is described as wearing dark-colored clothing.

questions.question: What is the unique feature of Sannanda's facial hair?
options: A long beard like a conchshell
questions.explanation: Sannanda is described as having two or three white hairs.

questions.question: To which Lord is Sannanda considered very dear?
questions.explanation: Sannanda is described as being very dear to Lord Kesava.

1.36.verse_text: Kuvalaya-devi is Sannanda's wife. Her complexion is the colour of a blue lotus flower and she wears red garments. Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower and his complexion is like the color of a peacock.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Kuvalaya-devi?
options: The color of a blue lotus flower
The color of a candata flower
questions.correct_answer: The color of a blue lotus flower
questions.explanation: Kuvalaya-devi is described as having a complexion the color of a blue lotus flower.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Kuvalaya-devi?
questions.explanation: Kuvalaya-devi is stated to wear red garments.

questions.question: Who is the husband of Kuvalaya-devi?
questions.explanation: Kuvalaya-devi is explicitly identified as the wife of Sannanda.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Nandana?
options: The color of a blue lotus flower
The color of a candata flower
questions.explanation: Nandana's complexion is described as resembling the color of a peacock.

questions.question: What color are the garments worn by Nandana?
options: The color of a candata flower
The color of a blue lotus flower
questions.correct_answer: The color of a candata flower
questions.explanation: Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower.

1.37.verse_text: Nandana lives in the same house with his father, Parjanya Maharaja. He is full of love for his yound nephew Krsna. Atulya-devi is Nandana's wife. Her complexion is like lightning and her garments resemble a dark cloud.

questions.question: With which relative does Nandana share a residence?
questions.explanation: Nandana is described as living in the same house as his father, Parjanya Maharaja.

questions.question: What is the nature of Nandana's internal feeling toward his young nephew, Krsna?
questions.explanation: Nandana is characterized as being full of love for his young nephew, Krsna.

questions.question: What is the name of Nandana's wife?
questions.explanation: Atulya-devi is identified as the wife of Nandana.

questions.question: Which natural phenomenon is compared to the complexion of Atulya-devi?
questions.explanation: Atulya-devi's complexion is described as being like lightning.

questions.question: What is the appearance of the garments worn by Atulya-devi?
options: They are the color of a candata flower
They are the color of a bandhujiva flower
They are of many different colors
questions.explanation: Atulya-devi is described as wearing garments that resemble a dark cloud.

1.38.verse_text: Krsna's father has two sisters, Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi. They both dress in garments of many different colors. They have beautiful teeth and their complexions are the color of white foam. Sananda-devi's husband is Mahanila and Nandini's husband is Sunila.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Nanda Maharaja's sister Sananda-devi?
questions.explanation: Sananda-devi, who is a sister of Krsna's father, is described as having a complexion the color of white foam.

questions.question: What is the name of the husband of Nanda Maharaja's sister Sananda-devi?
questions.explanation: Mahanila is identified as the husband of Sananda-devi.

1.39-40.verse_text: Kandava and Dandava are the two sons of Upananda. Their faces are as beautiful as lotus flowers and they become especially happy in the association of their friend Subala. Catu and Batuka are Nanda Maharaja's two ksatriya cousins born in Vasudeva Maharaja's dynasty. Catu's wife is Dadhisara and Batuka's wife is Havihsara.

questions.question: Who is the father of the gopas Kandava and Dandava?
questions.explanation: Kandava and Dandava are explicitly identified as the two sons of Upananda.

questions.question: Which friend of the cowherd boys Kandava and Dandava brings them great happiness?
questions.explanation: Kandava and Dandava are described as becoming especially happy in the association of their friend Subala.

questions.question: In which dynasty were Nanda Maharaja's cousins Catu and Batuka born?
questions.explanation: Catu and Batuka are identified as Nanda Maharaja's two ksatriya cousins who were born in the dynasty of Vasudeva Maharaja.

questions.question: Who is the wife of Nanda Maharaja's cousin Catu?
questions.explanation: Catu's wife is specifically named as Dadhisara.

questions.question: Who is the wife of Nanda Maharaja's cousin Batuka?
questions.explanation: Batuka's wife is specifically named as Havihsara.

1.41.verse_text: Krsna's energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather is named Sumukha. His long beard is like a great conchshell and his complexion is the color of a ripe jambu fruit.

questions.explanation: Sumukha is explicitly described as Krsna's energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather.

questions.explanation: Sumukha's long beard is specifically likened to a great conchshell.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Krsna's maternal grandfather, Sumukha?
options: The color of a ripe jambu fruit
The color of a golden champaka flower
The color of a fresh lotus petal
The color of a ripening mango
questions.correct_answer: The color of a ripe jambu fruit
questions.explanation: Sumukha is described as having a complexion the color of a ripe jambu fruit.

1.42.verse_text: Queen Patala-devi is Krsna's maternal grandmother and she is very famous in Vrajabhumi. Her hair is the white color of yogurt, her complexion is the pink color of a patala flower and her garments are green.

questions.question: What is the identity of Queen Patala-devi in relation to Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: Patala-devi is specifically described as Krsna's maternal grandmother.

questions.question: What is the distinctive color of Queen Patala-devi's hair?
questions.explanation: Her hair is described as having the white color of yogurt.

questions.question: Which specific shade best describes the complexion of Queen Patala-devi?
options: The pink color of a patala flower
The dark color of a ripe jambu fruit
The bright color of a red lotus
questions.correct_answer: The pink color of a patala flower
questions.explanation: Patala-devi's complexion is described as the pink color of a patala flower.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Queen Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Queen Patala-devi wears green garments.

1.43.verse_text: Patala-devi's dear friend is named Mukhara-gopi. Out of intense affection for her friend, Mukhara used to feed the infant Yasoda with her own breast milk.

questions.question: What is the name of the dear friend of Queen Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: Mukhara-gopi is explicitly identified as the dear friend of Queen Patala-devi.

questions.question: Which infant did Mukhara-gopi affectionately nourish with her own breast milk?
questions.explanation: Out of intense affection for her friend Patala-devi, Mukhara-gopi used to feed the infant Yasoda.

1.44-45.verse_text: Carumukha is Sumukha's younger brother. His complexion is the color of black eye-cosmetics and his wife Balaka-gopi was raised by her stepparents. Gola is the brother of Patala-devi, Krsna's maternal grandmother. His garments are the color of smoke. His brother-in-law, Sumukha, used to make fun of him and laugh and because of this Gola was very angry with him. Because he had worshiped Durvasa Muni in his previous birth, Gola was allowed to take birth in Vrajabhumi in the family of Ujjvala.

questions.question: What is the complexion of the gopa Carumukha?
options: The color of a bandhujiva flower
questions.explanation: Carumukha's complexion is described as being the color of black eye-cosmetics.

questions.question: Who is the elder brother of Carumukha?
questions.explanation: Carumukha is explicitly identified as the younger brother of Sumukha.

questions.question: Which gopi is the wife of Carumukha?
questions.explanation: Carumukha's wife is named Balaka-gopi, who was raised by her stepparents.

questions.question: What is the relationship of Gola to Krsna's maternal grandmother, Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: Gola is identified as the brother of Patala-devi, who is Krsna's maternal grandmother.

questions.question: What is the color of Gola's garments?
options: The color of a bandhujiva flower
questions.explanation: The text states that Gola's garments are the color of smoke.

questions.question: Which sage did Gola worship in his previous life to attain birth in the family of Ujjvala in Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: Gola took birth in the family of Ujjvala in Vrajabhumi as a result of worshiping Durvasa Muni in his previous birth.

questions.question: Why was Gola very angry with his brother-in-law, Sumukha?
options: Sumukha made fun of him and laughed
Sumukha refused to allow him into the family
questions.correct_answer: Sumukha made fun of him and laughed
questions.explanation: Gola was angry because his brother-in-law, Sumukha, used to mock him with laughter.

1.46.verse_text: Jatila-devi is Gola's wife. She has a large belly and her complexion is the color of a cow. Her sons Yasodhara, Yasodeva and Sudeva head the group of Krsna's maternal uncles.

questions.question: Who is the wife of Gola?
questions.explanation: Jatila-devi is identified as the wife of Gola.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Jatila-devi?
options: The color of a dark atasi flower
questions.explanation: Jatila-devi's complexion is specifically described as being the color of a cow.

options: She has a very tall stature
She has a fair, golden complexion
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Jatila-devi has a large belly.

questions.question: Who are the three sons of Jatila-devi?
questions.explanation: Yasodhara, Yasodeva, and Sudeva are listed as the sons of Jatila-devi.

questions.question: What position do Yasodhara, Yasodeva, and Sudeva hold among Krsna's relatives?
options: They are the leaders of Krsna's friends
They head the group of Krsna's maternal uncles
They are the chief cowherd guards of Vraja
They are the primary personal servants of Krsna
questions.correct_answer: They head the group of Krsna's maternal uncles
questions.explanation: Yasodhara, Yasodeva, and Sudeva are identified as the leaders of Krsna's maternal uncles.

1.47.verse_text: Krsna's maternal uncles have complexions like the color of a dark atasi flower and they dress in white garments. Their wives have complexions like karkati flowers and they dress in smoke-colored garments.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Krsna's maternal uncles?
options: Color of a dark atasi flower
Color of a golden champaka flower

options: Color of a dark atasi flower
Color of a fresh blade of grass
questions.explanation: The wives of the maternal uncles of Krsna are described as having complexions like karkati flowers.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by the wives of Krsna's maternal uncles?
questions.explanation: The verse states that the wives of Krsna's maternal uncles dress in smoke-colored garments.

1.48.verse_text: Rema-devi, Roma-devi and Surema-devi are the daughters of Krsna's parental uncle Pavana. Yasodevi and Yasasvini are the sisters of Krsna's mother Yasoda. Yasasvini's husband is named Malla.

questions.question: Which gopis are recognized as the daughters of Krsna's parental uncle Pavana?
questions.explanation: Rema-devi, Roma-devi, and Surema-devi are explicitly identified as the three daughters of Pavana, who is Krsna's parental uncle.

questions.question: What is the name of the parental uncle of Krsna who is the father of Rema-devi, Roma-devi, and Surema-devi?
questions.explanation: Pavana is specifically identified as the parental uncle of Krsna and the father of Rema-devi, Roma-devi, and Surema-devi.

questions.question: Who are the two sisters of Krsna's mother, Mother Yasoda?

questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Malla as the husband of Yasasvini, who is the sister of Krsna's mother.

1.49.verse_text: Yasodevi is elder. Her complexion is dark and she is also known by the name Dadhisara. Yasasvini is younger. Her complexion is fair and she is also known by the name Havihsara. Both dress in vermillion-colored garments.

questions.explanation: Yasodevi is identified as the elder sister, while Yasasvini is the younger.

questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the elder sister Yasodevi has a dark complexion.

questions.question: By what other name is Yasodevi, the elder sister of Yasoda, known?
questions.explanation: Yasodevi is also known by the name Dadhisara.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Yasoda's younger sister, Yasasvini?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Yasasvini is the younger sister and possesses a fair complexion.

questions.question: By what other name is Yasasvini, the younger sister of Yasoda, known?
questions.explanation: Yasasvini is also known by the name Havihsara.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by both Yasodevi and Yasasvini?
questions.explanation: Both Yasodevi and Yasasvini are described as dressing in vermillion-colored garments.

1.50.verse_text: Yasoda's sisters married the two ksatriyas Catu and Batuka (see text 40). Yasoda's uncle Carumukha has one son who is named Sucaru.

1.56-58.verse_text: Mangala, Pingala, Pinga, Mathura, Pitha, Pattisa, Sankara, Sangara, Bhrngaq, Ghrni, Ghatika, Saragha, Pathira, Dandi, Kedara, Saurabheya, Kala, Ankura, Dhurina, Dhurva, Cakranga, Maskara, Utpala, Kambala, Supaksa, Saudha, Harita, Harikesa, Hara and Upananda are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's father, Nanda Maharaja.

questions.explanation: Mangala is explicitly mentioned as one of the contemporary associates of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: Among the following cowherd associates of Nanda Maharaja, who is included in the list of his contemporaries?
questions.explanation: Pitha is identified as a contemporary associate of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.explanation: Bhrnga is recorded as a contemporary associate of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.explanation: Dhurina is included in the list of Nanda Maharaja's contemporary associates.

1.59.verse_text: Having taken a vow of friendship in their youth, Parjanya and Sumukha always remain the best of friends. Nanda Maharaja and many other of the cowherds are descended from their families.

questions.question: Who is the eternal best friend of Parjanya Maharaja, with whom he took a vow of friendship in their youth?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Sumukha as the one with whom Parjanya took a vow of friendship in their youth, establishing them as the best of friends.

questions.question: From whose lineage are Nanda Maharaja and the other prominent cowherds of Vraja said to be descended?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Nanda Maharaja and many other cowherds are descended from the families of Parjanya and Sumukha.

1.60-62.verse_text: Vatsala, Kusala, Tali, Medura, Masrna, Krpa, Sankini, Bimbini, Mitra, Subhaga, Bhogini, Prabha, Sarika Hingula, Niti, Kapila, Dhamanidhara, Paksati, Pataka, Pundi, Sutunda, Tusti, Anjana, Tarangaksi, Taralika, Subhada, Malika, Angada, Visala, Sallaki, Vena and Vartika are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's mother, Yasoda-devi.

questions.question: Which of these cowherd women is listed among the contemporary associates of Krsna's mother, Yasoda-devi?
questions.explanation: Vatsala is explicitly mentioned as one of the contemporary associates of Mother Yasoda.

questions.question: Which of the following figures is identified as a companion of Mother Yasoda within the Vraja circle?
questions.explanation: Medura is clearly named as an associate belonging to the generation of Yasoda-devi.

questions.question: Among the friends of Yasoda-devi mentioned in the Vraja records, which name is included?
questions.explanation: Sankini is listed as one of the specific contemporaries and associates of Mother Yasoda.

1.63.verse_text: Ambika and Kilimba are the two nurses who fed Krsna with their breast milk. Of the two, Ambika, the dear friend of Vraja's queen, is the most important.

questions.question: Who are the two nurses described as having fed Krsna with their breast milk?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Ambika and Kilimba as the two nurses who provided breast milk to Krsna.

questions.question: Which of the two nurses, Ambika or Kilimba, is considered the most important and a dear friend of Queen Yasoda?
questions.explanation: Ambika is explicitly described as the most important of the two nurses and a dear friend of Vraja's queen, Yasoda-devi.

1.64-65.verse_text: The Brahmanas: Two groups of brahmanas reside in Gokula : those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those who are priests engaged in the performance of Vedic sacrifices. Among the priests, Vedagarbha, Mahayajva and Bhaguri are prominent. Their respective wives are Samadheni, Mahakavya and Vedika.

questions.question: Which of the following is identified as one of the prominent priests in Gokula who performs Vedic sacrifices for the residents of Vraja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Vedagarbha, Mahayajva, and Bhaguri as the prominent priests engaged in Vedic sacrifices.

questions.question: Who is the wife of the prominent priest Mahayajva?
questions.explanation: The text lists Mahakavya as the wife of the priest Mahayajva.

questions.question: What is the name of the wife of the priest Bhaguri?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Vedika as the wife of the priest Bhaguri.

questions.question: Which priest is known to be the husband of Samadheni?
questions.explanation: The text pairs Vedagarbha with his wife, Samadheni.

questions.question: In what two categories are the brahmanas of Gokula divided according to their roles?
options: Those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those engaged in Vedic sacrifices
Those from Mathura and those from Vrindavana
Those who are ministers and those who are merchants
Those who are elderly and those who are young
questions.correct_answer: Those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those engaged in Vedic sacrifices
questions.explanation: The text describes the two groups of brahmanas as those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those who are priests engaged in the performance of Vedic sacrifices.

1.66.verse_text: Sulabha, Gautami, Gargi, Candila, Kubjika, Vamani, Svaha, Sulata, Sandili, Svadha and Bhargavi are the most important of the brahmana ladies respectfully worshiped by the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: Which of these brahmana ladies is identified as one of the most prominent ones respected by the residents of Vraja?
questions.explanation: Sulabha is explicitly listed as one of the most important brahmana ladies worshiped by the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: Which lady is counted among the important brahmana women in Vraja along with Gargi and Candila?
questions.explanation: Gautami is mentioned alongside Gargi and Candila as a respected brahmana lady of Vraja.

questions.question: Among the list of respected brahmana ladies in Vraja, which of the following is included?
questions.explanation: Kubjika is listed as one of the prominent brahmana ladies worshiped by the inhabitants of Vraja.

questions.question: Which of these women is identified as a respected brahmana lady of Vraja in the same category as Vamani and Svaha?
questions.explanation: Sulata is mentioned as a significant brahmana lady alongside Vamani and Svaha.

questions.question: Which of the following is listed as a venerable brahmana lady residing in Vraja, alongside Sandili and Svadha?
questions.explanation: Bhargavi is listed as one of the eleven most important brahmana ladies respected in Vraja.

1.67-69.verse_text: Full of transcendental opulences, Paurnamasi-devi is the incarnation of the Lord's Yogamaya potency. She makes the arrangements of Lord Krsna's pastimes to be properly performed. She is slightly tall in stature and has a fair complexion. Her hair is the color of kasa flowers and she wears red garments. She is greatly respected by Nanda Maharaja and all the other residents of Vrajabhumi. She was the dear student of Devarsi Narada and on his advice she left her favorite son, Sandipani Muni, in Avantipura and came to Gokula, impelled by great love for her worshipable Lord Krsna.

questions.question: Paurnamasi-devi is known as the incarnation of which specific potency of the Lord?
questions.explanation: Paurnamasi-devi is identified as the incarnation of the Lord's Yogamaya potency.

questions.question: What is the complexion of the great devotee Paurnamasi-devi?
questions.explanation: Paurnamasi-devi is described as having a fair complexion.

questions.question: What is the unique color of the hair of Paurnamasi-devi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Paurnamasi-devi's hair as having the color of kasa flowers.

1.70.verse_text: Krsna's associates may be divided into two groups : gopis and gopas. The gopis may be divided into three groups : 1. Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna, 2. maidservants and 3. gopi messengers.

questions.question: What are the two primary categories into which the associates of Lord Krsna are divided?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Krsna's associates are divided into these two groups: gopis and gopas.

questions.question: Into how many specific groups are the gopis categorized?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies three groups of gopis: gopi friends of the same age, maidservants, and gopi messengers.

questions.question: Which of the following is listed as one of the three divisions of the gopis?
questions.explanation: The text identifies gopi messengers as one of the three specific divisions of the gopis.

questions.question: What specific group of gopis is described alongside maidservants and messengers?
options: Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna
Gopis who are elders of Vraja
Gopis who are wives of the cowherd men
questions.correct_answer: Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna
questions.explanation: The verse defines the three groups of gopis as friends of the same age, maidservants, and messengers.

1.71-72.verse_text: Learned scholars have also divided Lord Krsna's associates into the following nine categories : 1. yutha, 2. kula, 3. mandala, 4. varga, 5. gana, 6. samavaya, 7. sancaya, 8. samaja, and 9. and samanvaya.

questions.question: Which category among the nine groups of Lord Krsna's associates corresponds to the gopi friends who are of the same age as the Lord?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the group of Krsna's contemporary gopi friends as corresponding to the samaja category.

questions.question: Which classification of Krsna's associates is associated with the maidservants?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the second group of associates, the maidservants, as corresponding to the mandala category.

questions.question: Which of the nine categories listed for Lord Krsna's associates corresponds to the gopi messengers?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the third group, the gopi messengers, as corresponding to the gana category.

1.73-75.verse_text: Krsna's Gopi Friends : Of the three kinds of gopi associates of the Lord mentioned in text 70, the first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, are the most exalted, the second group, the maidservants are the next most exalted, and the gopi messengers come after them. These three groups correspond to the groups mentioned as samaja, mandala and gana respectively (in texts 71 - 72). The first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, may be further divided into varistha (the most exalted ) and vara (exalted).

questions.question: Which specific category of gopi associates is considered the most exalted among the three groups?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's contemporary gopi friends are the most exalted of the three kinds of gopi associates.

questions.question: Which of the following designations corresponds to the group of gopi messengers?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that the gopi messengers correspond to the category of gana.

questions.question: The second group of gopi associates, the maidservants, is identified by which category name?
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the gopi maidservants correspond to the mandala category.

questions.question: Which specific category name refers to Krsna's contemporary gopi friends?
questions.explanation: The text states that the first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, corresponds to the samaja category.

questions.question: Into which two further divisions can the varistha and vara gopis be categorized?
questions.explanation: The text explains that the first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, is further divided into varistha (most exalted) and vara (exalted).

1.76.verse_text: Varistha (The Most Exalted Gopis): The varistha gopis are more famous than all the others. They are eternally the intimate friends of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna. No one can equal or exceed the love they bear for the divine couple.

questions.question: Which specific group of gopis is described as being more famous than all others in the confidential associates of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna?
questions.explanation: The varistha gopis are explicitly identified as the most famous among all other groups of gopi associates.

questions.question: What is the eternal relationship of the varistha gopis with the divine couple Sri Sri Radha-Krsna?
options: They are eternally the intimate friends
They are eternally the humble maidservants
They are eternally the royal messengers
They are eternally the distant well-wishers
questions.correct_answer: They are eternally the intimate friends
questions.explanation: The varistha gopis are described as the eternal intimate friends of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna.

questions.question: How is the depth of love borne by the varistha gopis for the divine couple described in relation to others?
options: No one can equal or exceed it
It is equal to the love of the cowherd men
It is surpassed by the love of the maidservants
It is secondary to the love of the gopi messengers

questions.explanation: The text states that no one can equal or exceed the love that the varistha gopis bear for the divine couple.

1.77.verse_text: Of all Krsna's friends, the varistha gopis are the most worshipable. They are decorated with incomparable transcendental qualities, bodily beauty and all other charming opulences.

questions.question: Which specific group among all of Krsna's friends is considered the most worshipable?

options: Incomparable transcendental qualities and all other charming opulences
Wealth, kingdom, and command over the demons of Vraja
The ability to assume any form and travel through the spiritual sky
Unending knowledge of the Vedas and mastery of the sixty-four arts

questions.explanation: The verse confirms they are decorated with incomparable transcendental qualities, bodily beauty, and all other charming opulences.

1.79.verse_text: Lalita: Of these eight gopis, the first one, Lalita-devi, is the best. She is a dear friend of the divine couple and she is 27 years old, the eldest of Krsna's gopi friends.

The shimmering colors of peacock feathers
The rays of the rising sun

1.82-83.verse_text: Visakha: Visakha is the second most important of these eight varistha-gopis. Her attributes, activities and resolve are all muck like those of her friend Lalita. Visakha was born at the exact same moment as her dear friend Srimati Radharani appeared in this world. Visakha's garments are decorated with stars and her complexion is like lightning. Her father is Pavana, the son of the sister of Mukhara-gopi and her mother is Daksina-devi, the daughter of the sister of Jatila. Her husband is Vahika-gopa.
