questions.explanation: The text states that some of Indulekha-devi's friends are specifically engaged in providing ornaments for the divine couple.

questions.question: Besides providing ornaments, what specific material contribution do some of Indulekha-devi's friends offer the divine couple?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that others among Indulekha-devi's friends provide exquisite garments for the divine couple.

questions.question: Which responsibility is entrusted to the friends of Indulekha-devi regarding the divine couple's resources?
questions.explanation: It is mentioned that certain friends of Indulekha-devi are tasked with guarding the divine couple's treasury.

1.193.verse_text: Ranga-devi: Ranga-devi is always like a great ocean of coquettish words and gestures. She is very fond of joking with her friend Srimati Radharani in the presence of Lord Krishna.

questions.question: Ranga-devi is metaphorically described as a great ocean of what specific types of expressions?
questions.explanation: Ranga-devi is characterized as an ocean of coquettish words and gestures.

questions.question: With whom does Ranga-devi habitually enjoy joking while in the company of Lord Krishna?
questions.explanation: Ranga-devi is fond of joking with her friend Srimati Radharani in the presence of Lord Krishna.

1.194.verse_text: Among the six activities of diplomacy she is especially expert in the fourth : patiently waiting for the enemy to make the next move. She is an expert logician and because of previous austerities she has attained a mantra by which she can attract Lord Krsna.

questions.question: Which specific activity among the six types of diplomacy is Ranga-devi noted for being especially expert in?
options: Patiently waiting for the enemy to make the next move
questions.correct_answer: Patiently waiting for the enemy to make the next move

questions.question: Besides diplomacy, in what specific field of intellectual skill is Ranga-devi described as being an expert?

questions.question: What is the source of Ranga-devi's ability to attract Lord Krishna according to the description of her past?
questions.explanation: Ranga-devi has attained a special mantra to attract Lord Krishna specifically because of the results of her previous austerities.

1.195.verse_text: Kalakanti-devi is the leader of the eight most important friends of Ranga-devi. These friends are all expert in the use of perfumes and cosmetics.

1.196.verse_text: Ranga-devi's friends are expert at burning aromatic incense, carrying coal during the winter and fanning the divine couple in the summer.

questions.question: What is the primary expertise of the friends of Ranga-devi regarding aromatic substances?

1.197.verse_text: Ranga-devi's friends are able to control the lions, deer and other wild animals in the forest. Ranga-devi is the leader of all these gopis.

options: They can make the wild animals fall into a deep sleep
They are able to control lions, deer, and other wild animals

They can command the tigers to dance for the divine couple

1.198.verse_text: Sudevi: Sudevi always remains at the side of her dear friend Srimati Radharani. Sudevi arranges Radharani's hair, decorates Her eyes with mascara and massages Her body.

questions.question: Which constant service does Sudevi perform for Srimati Radharani regarding Her hair?
options: Weaving Her hair into a braid
Decorating Her hair with fresh jasmine flowers
Applying fragrant oils to Her hair
questions.explanation: Sudevi is specifically described as the one who arranges the hair of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: Besides arranging hair and massaging the body, what other specific service does Sudevi perform for the beauty of Srimati Radharani?
options: Applying sandal paste to Her arms
Painting artistic designs on Her cheeks
Adorning Her feet with red lac dye
questions.explanation: The text states that Sudevi decorates Srimati Radharani's eyes with mascara.

questions.question: Where is the position of Sudevi in relation to Srimati Radharani at all times?
options: Following behind Her as a shadow
Always remaining at the side of Her
Waiting at the entrance of the kunja
Standing before Her with folded palms
questions.correct_answer: Always remaining at the side of Her
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Sudevi always remains at the side of her dear friend Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: Which of the following physical services does Sudevi regularly perform for Srimati Radharani?
options: Fanning Her with a peacock fan
questions.explanation: The description identifies that Sudevi massages the body of Srimati Radharani.

1.199.verse_text: She is expert in training male and female parrots and she is also expert in the pastimes of roosters. She is an expert sailor and she is fully aware of the auspicious and inauspicious omens described in the Sakuna-sastra.

questions.question: Besides training parrots, in which specific animal pastimes is the gopi Sudevi highly expert?
questions.explanation: Sudevi is described as being expert in the pastimes of roosters, along with her ability to train both male and female parrots.

questions.question: What nautical skill is attributed to Sudevi to demonstrate her versatility in Vraja-lila?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Sudevi is an expert sailor.

questions.question: What specialized knowledge does Sudevi possess regarding the interpretation of signs and omens?
questions.explanation: Sudevi is described as being fully aware of the auspicious and inauspicious omens as they are detailed in the Sakuna-sastra.

1.200.verse_text: She is expert at massaging the body with scented oils, she knows how to start fires and keep them burning and she knows which flowers blossom with the rising of the moon.

questions.question: Besides being an expert sailor and parrot trainer, which skill does Sudevi possess regarding the physical care of the Divine Couple?
options: Massaging the body with scented oils
Arranging floral garlands for the bed
Combing the hair of Srimati Radharani
Applying collyrium to the lotus eyes

questions.explanation: Sudevi is described as being expert at massaging the body with scented oils.

questions.question: Which specific expertise does Sudevi have regarding the maintenance of warmth and domestic light in the Vraja-pastimes?
options: Knowing how to start fires and keep them burning
Churning butter for the evening meal
Polishing the brass lamps in the nikunja
questions.correct_answer: Knowing how to start fires and keep them burning
questions.explanation: Sudevi is noted for her expertise in starting fires and keeping them burning.

questions.question: Which botanical knowledge does Sudevi possess to aid the intimate service of Srimati Radharani?
options: Knowing which flowers blossom with the rising of the moon
Knowing which creepers produce the most fragrant nectar
Knowing which leaves are best for constructing spitoons
Knowing which blossoms are favored by the honeybees
questions.correct_answer: Knowing which flowers blossom with the rising of the moon
questions.explanation: Sudevi is knowledgeable about which specific flowers bloom when the moon rises.

1.201.verse_text: Kaveri-devi and the other friends of Sudevi are expert at constructing leaf-spitoons, playing music on bells and decorating couches in various ways.

questions.question: Besides being friends of Sudevi, what is the primary expertise of Kaveri-devi and her companions regarding the assembly of various items?
questions.explanation: Kaveri-devi and the other friends of Sudevi are specifically described as being expert at constructing leaf-spitoons.

questions.question: What unique musical talent do Kaveri-devi and the companions of Sudevi possess?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kaveri-devi and the friends of Sudevi are expert at playing music on bells.

questions.question: In which specific domestic art are Kaveri-devi and the other friends of Sudevi considered highly proficient?
questions.explanation: The verse credits Kaveri-devi and the friends of Sudevi with the expertise of decorating couches in various ways.

1.202-203.verse_text: Sudevi's friends are also entrusted with the decoration of the divine couple's sitting place. Sudevi's friends act as clever spies, disguising themselves in various ways and moving among Radharani's rivals (Candravali and her friends) to discover their secrets. Sudevi's friends are the deities of Vrndavana forest and they are charged with the protection of the forest birds and bees. Sudevi is the leader of these gopis.

questions.question: Besides decorating the couch, for which specific task are the friends of Sudevi entrusted?
options: Preparing the divine couple's sitting place
Arranging the flowers for the forest entrance
Carrying the water pots from the Yamuna
Tending to the cows in the pasture
questions.correct_answer: Preparing the divine couple's sitting place
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the friends of Sudevi are entrusted with the decoration of the divine couple's sitting place.

questions.question: What specialized role do the friends of Sudevi perform when moving among the rivals of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text describes how the friends of Sudevi act as clever spies, disguising themselves to discover the secrets of Candravali and her friends.

questions.question: Which gopi serves as the leader for the group of friends who act as the deities of Vrndavana forest?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly states that Sudevi is the leader of these gopis who are charged with the protection of the forest birds and bees.

questions.question: What is the primary responsibility of the friends of Sudevi in their capacity as the deities of Vrndavana forest?
options: The protection of the forest birds and bees
The milking of the surabhi cows
The cleaning of the sacred Yamuna ghats
questions.correct_answer: The protection of the forest birds and bees
questions.explanation: It is mentioned that Sudevi's friends are the deities of the forest and are specifically charged with the protection of the forest birds and bees.

1.204.verse_text: Description of the Various Gopis: The wonderful arts, crafts and other duties that comprise the activities of the gopis will now be described.

1.205-206.verse_text: Pindaka-devi, Nirvitandika-devi, Pundarika-devi, Sitakhandi-devi, Carucandi-devi, Sudantika-devi, Akunthita-devi, Kalakanthi-devi, Ramaci-devi and Mecika-devi are among the gopis who are very strong and stubborn when there is an argument or conflict. Among them Pindaka-devi is the leader. She dresses in red garments. She is very beautiful. When Lord Krsna comes she embarrasses Him by attacking Him with many ferocious witty puns.

questions.question: Which gopi is recognized as the leader among the group of gopis known for their strength and stubbornness during arguments?
questions.explanation: Pindaka-devi is explicitly identified as the leader among the group including Nirvitandika, Pundarika, Sitakhandi, Carucandi, Sudantika, Akunthita, Kalakanthi, Ramaci, and Mecika.

questions.question: What is the specific color of the garments worn by Pindaka-devi?
questions.explanation: The text states that Pindaka-devi dresses in red garments.

questions.question: By what means does Pindaka-devi typically embarrass Lord Krsna upon His arrival?
options: By teasing Him about Srimati Radharani
By attacking Him with ferocious witty puns
By singing songs of His pastimes
questions.correct_answer: By attacking Him with ferocious witty puns
questions.explanation: Pindaka-devi embarrasses Lord Krsna when He comes by attacking Him with many ferocious witty puns.

questions.question: Which gopi is listed alongside Pindaka-devi, Nirvitandika-devi, and Pundarika-devi as being known for a strong and stubborn nature in conflict?
questions.explanation: Carucandi-devi is included in the list of gopis who are strong and stubborn during arguments or conflicts.

1.207.verse_text: Haridrabha-devi, Hariccela-devi and Harimitra-devi speak many illogical and frivolous objections as they lead Lord Krsna to the place where Srimati Radharani waits for Him.

questions.question: Which specific gopis are known for speaking illogical and frivolous objections while leading Lord Krsna to the meeting place with Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: These three gopis are specifically identified for their frivolous objections while guiding Krsna to Radharani.

questions.question: What is the primary service performed by Haridrabha-devi, Hariccela-devi, and Harimitra-devi when they are with Lord Krsna?
options: Leading Him to the place where Srimati Radharani is waiting
Adorning Him with forest flowers and sandalwood paste
Guarding the entrance to the forest groves
Entertaining Him with music and dance during the rasa-lila
questions.correct_answer: Leading Him to the place where Srimati Radharani is waiting
questions.explanation: The verse describes them as leading Lord Krsna to the location of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: How do Haridrabha-devi, Hariccela-devi, and Harimitra-devi behave while escorting Lord Krsna to see Srimati Radharani?
options: They speak many illogical and frivolous objections
They offer prayers and sing the glories of His beauty
They remain silent and maintain strict decorum
They perform an elaborate dance to delight Him
questions.correct_answer: They speak many illogical and frivolous objections
questions.explanation: The text characterizes their interaction with Krsna as consisting of illogical and frivolous objections.

1.208.verse_text: Pundarika-devi's garments and complexion are both the color of a white lotus flower. She ferociously mocks lotus-eyed Lord Krsna.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Pundarika-devi?
questions.explanation: Pundarika-devi's garments are described as being the color of a white lotus flower.

questions.question: Which specific complexion does Pundarika-devi possess?
questions.explanation: The text states that Pundarika-devi's complexion is the color of a white lotus flower.

questions.question: How does Pundarika-devi behave toward the lotus-eyed Lord Krsna?
options: She sings sweet songs of praise
She hides Him from the elders
questions.explanation: It is noted that Pundarika-devi ferociously mocks the lotus-eyed Lord Krsna.

1.209.verse_text: Gauri-devi always wears white garments. Her complexion is the color of a peacock. Because her sweet and charming words are often laced with acid sarcasms, Lord Krsna jokingly calls her Sitakhandi (always sweet).

questions.question: What is the specific color of the garments worn by Gauri-devi?
questions.explanation: Gauri-devi is described as always wearing white garments.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Gauri-devi?
questions.explanation: The complexion of Gauri-devi is stated to be the color of a peacock.

questions.question: By what humorous nickname does Lord Krsna address Gauri-devi due to her acid sarcasms?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna jokingly calls Gauri-devi 'Sitakhandi', which implies she is 'always sweet', as a reaction to her acid-laced, charming words.

1.210.verse_text: Gauri-devi's sister is known as Carucandi because her words are sometimes mild and pleasant (caru) and sometimes harsh and violent (candi). Carucandi's complexion is the color of a black bee and her garments are the color of lightning.

questions.question: What is the reason given for the name of the gopi Carucandi?
options: She is both beautiful and cruel in her nature
Her words are sometimes mild and pleasant and sometimes harsh and violent
She is the sister of Gauri-devi and has a charming personality
She possesses a radiant complexion and fiery temperament
questions.correct_answer: Her words are sometimes mild and pleasant and sometimes harsh and violent
questions.explanation: The name Carucandi is derived from her nature of having speech that is both mild/pleasant (caru) and harsh/violent (candi).

questions.question: What is the complexion of Carucandi?
options: The color of a black bee
The color of a sirisa flower
The color of a bandhujiva flower
questions.correct_answer: The color of a black bee
questions.explanation: Carucandi is described as having a complexion matching the color of a black bee.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Carucandi?
options: The color of a kurunthaka flower
The color of a white cloud
questions.explanation: Carucandi is described as wearing garments that are the color of lightning.

questions.question: Who is the sister of the gopi Gauri-devi?
questions.explanation: Carucandi is explicitly identified as the sister of Gauri-devi.

1.211.verse_text: Sudantika-devi wears garments the color of a kurunthaka flower. Her complexion is the color of a sirisa flower. She is expert at inflaming the amorous sentiments of ujjvala-rasa.

questions.question: What is the specific color of the garments worn by Sudantika-devi?
questions.explanation: Sudantika-devi is described as wearing garments the color of a kurunthaka flower.

questions.question: Which flower best describes the complexion of Sudantika-devi?
questions.explanation: Sudantika-devi's complexion is explicitly compared to the color of a sirisa flower.

questions.question: In what specific area is Sudantika-devi expert regarding the pastimes of the Divine Couple?
options: Inflaming the amorous sentiments of ujjvala-rasa
Composing verses to praise the beauty of Krsna
Arranging the secret meetings of Radha and Krsna
Teaching the gopis how to play the flute
questions.correct_answer: Inflaming the amorous sentiments of ujjvala-rasa
questions.explanation: Sudantika-devi is noted for her expertise in inflaming the amorous sentiments of ujjvala-rasa.

1.212.verse_text: Akunthita-devi's complexion is the color of a lotus stem. She wears white garments the color of the fibers within a lotus stem. She likes to insult Krsna for the amusement of her gopi friends.

questions.question: What is the specific color of Akunthita-devi's complexion?
questions.explanation: Akunthita-devi's complexion is described as being the color of a lotus stem.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Akunthita-devi?
questions.explanation: Akunthita-devi wears white garments that match the color of the fibers within a lotus stem.

questions.question: For whose amusement does Akunthita-devi often insult Krsna?
options: For the amusement of her gopi friends
For the amusement of Mother Yasoda
For the amusement of the elderly gopis
For the amusement of the forest animals
questions.correct_answer: For the amusement of her gopi friends
questions.explanation: Akunthita-devi enjoys insulting Krsna to provide amusement for her gopi friends.

1.213.verse_text: Kalakanthi-devi's complexion is the color of a kuli flower and her garments are the color of milk. She speaks to Lord Krnsa, describing Radharani's jealous anger and advising Him to beg forgiveness from Her.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Kalakanthi-devi?
options: The color of a lotus stem
The color of a kuli flower
The color of a golden champa
The color of a monsoon cloud
questions.correct_answer: The color of a kuli flower
questions.explanation: Kalakanthi-devi's complexion is explicitly described as the color of a kuli flower.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Kalakanthi-devi?
questions.explanation: Kalakanthi-devi is described as wearing garments the color of milk.

questions.question: What is the specific nature of the conversation Kalakanthi-devi has with Lord Krsna regarding Srimati Radharani?
options: She tells Krsna to hide from Radharani's sight
She describes Radharani's jealous anger and advises Krsna to beg for forgiveness
She mocks Krsna for His inability to find Radharani
She delivers a secret message from the gopis to Krsna
questions.correct_answer: She describes Radharani's jealous anger and advises Krsna to beg for forgiveness
questions.explanation: Kalakanthi-devi's service in Vraja-lila includes informing Krsna of Radharani's jealous anger and advising Him to seek Her forgiveness.

1.214.verse_text: Ramaci-devi's is the daughter of Lalita-devi's nurse. Ramaci's complexion is golden and her garments are the color of a parrot. She takes pleasure in jokingly insulting Krsna and laughing at Him.

questions.question: Who is the mother of Ramaci-devi?
questions.explanation: Ramaci-devi is explicitly identified as the daughter of Lalita-devi's nurse.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Ramaci-devi?
questions.explanation: Ramaci-devi's complexion is described as golden.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Ramaci-devi?
questions.explanation: Ramaci-devi is described as wearing garments that are the color of a parrot.

questions.question: Which specific pastime of jokingly insulting and laughing at Krsna is attributed to Ramaci-devi?
options: Jokingly insulting and laughing at Krsna
Teasing Him about His peacock feather
questions.correct_answer: Jokingly insulting and laughing at Krsna
questions.explanation: It is stated that Ramaci-devi takes pleasure in jokingly insulting Krsna and laughing at Him.

1.215.verse_text: Ramaci-devi always wears white garments. Her complexion is the color of a pinda flower. She is expert at insulting Lord Krnsa.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Ramaci-devi?
questions.explanation: Ramaci-devi is explicitly described as always wearing white garments.

questions.question: What is the complexion of Ramaci-devi?
options: The color of a golden champaka flower
The color of a pinda flower
The color of a bandhujiva flower
The color of a blue lotus
questions.correct_answer: The color of a pinda flower
questions.explanation: Ramaci-devi's complexion is described as having the color of a pinda flower.

questions.question: In what activity is Ramaci-devi considered to be expert?
options: Arranging for the divine couple's meal
questions.explanation: Ramaci-devi is specifically noted for her expertise in insulting Lord Krsna.

1.216-217.verse_text: Petari-devi, Varuda-devi, Cari-devi, Kotari-devi, Kalatippani-devi, Marunda-devi, Morata-devi, Cuda-devi, Cundari-devi and Gondika-devi are the leaders of those gopi messengers who are past the prime of youth. These older gopis can argue with great stubbornness and they can also nicely sing as the divine couple take their meal. These gopis are always engaged in making arrangements for the forest pastimes of the divine couple.

questions.question: Which specific group of gopi messengers is led by Petari-devi, Varuda-devi, and their associates?
options: The gopis who are eternally young
The gopis who are past the prime of youth
The gopis who are currently performing rasa-lila
questions.correct_answer: The gopis who are past the prime of youth
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Petari-devi and her companions as the leaders of the gopi messengers who have passed the prime of their youth.

questions.question: Besides their role as messengers, what unique skill do the older gopis led by Petari-devi and Varuda-devi exhibit while the Divine Couple takes their meal?
options: They expertly arrange the flower beds
They fan the Divine Couple with peacock feathers
questions.explanation: The text states that these older gopis can sing nicely while the Divine Couple is taking their meal.

questions.question: What primary service do the elderly gopis like Kotari-devi and Marunda-devi perform regarding the forest pastimes of Radha and Krsna?
options: Decorating the Divine Couple with forest flowers
Making arrangements for the forest pastimes
Preparing the meals consumed in the forest
questions.correct_answer: Making arrangements for the forest pastimes
questions.explanation: The verse identifies that these gopis are always engaged in making arrangements for the forest pastimes of the divine couple.

questions.question: What is a notable personality trait of the older gopi messengers led by Cuda-devi and Cundari-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse describes these older gopis as being able to argue with great stubbornness.

1.218.verse_text: Petari-devi is an elderly Gujarati lady whose hair is the color of fibers within a lotus stem. Varudi-devi is a native of Garuda-desa and her braided hair is like the current of a river.

questions.question: What is the cultural background of the elderly gopi messenger Petari-devi?
questions.explanation: Petari-devi is described specifically as an elderly Gujarati lady.

questions.question: To what are the hair fibers of Petari-devi compared in color?
questions.explanation: Petari-devi's hair color is compared to the fibers found within a lotus stem.

questions.question: What is the native land of the gopi messenger Varudi-devi?
questions.explanation: Varudi-devi is described as being a native of Garuda-desa.

questions.question: To what is the appearance of Varudi-devi's braided hair compared?
options: The white foam of the Yamuna
The fiber of a lotus stem
questions.explanation: Varudi-devi's braided hair is described as resembling the current of a river.

1.219.verse_text: Cari-devi, who is Kucari-devi's siste, is also known by the name Tapahkatyayani, because she performed severe austerities (tapah) and took shelter of goddess Katyayani (Durga). Kotari-devi was born in the abhira caste. Her hair is a mixture of black and white resembling rice mixed with toasted sesame seeds.

questions.question: What is another name for the associate known as Cari-devi?
questions.explanation: Cari-devi is known as Tapahkatyayani due to her severe austerities and her shelter of goddess Katyayani.

questions.question: To whom is Cari-devi related as a sister?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Cari-devi as the sister of Kucari-devi.

questions.question: In which community was Kotari-devi born?
questions.explanation: Kotari-devi is specifically described as being born in the abhira caste.

questions.question: The hair color of Kotari-devi is compared to a mixture of which two items?
questions.explanation: Kotari-devi's hair is described as black and white, resembling a mixture of rice and toasted sesame seeds.

1.220.verse_text: Kalatippani-devi is an elderly washerwoman with white hair. Marunda-devi has white eyebrows and a shaved head.

questions.question: What is the specific occupation of the elderly associate Kalatippani-devi?
questions.explanation: Kalatippani-devi is explicitly described as an elderly washerwoman.

questions.question: What is the color of Kalatippani-devi's hair?
questions.explanation: Kalatippani-devi is described as having white hair.

questions.question: What is the unique physical appearance of Marunda-devi's head?
questions.explanation: Marunda-devi is described as having a shaved head.

questions.question: What is the color of the eyebrows of the associate Marunda-devi?
questions.explanation: Marunda-devi is specifically noted to have white eyebrows.

1.221.verse_text: Agile Morata-devi has hair the color of a kasa flower. Cuda-devi has a wrinkled face and a forehead decorated with many grey hairs.

questions.question: What is the distinctive hair color of the agile Morata-devi?
questions.explanation: Morata-devi is described as being agile and possessing hair that resembles the color of a kasa flower.

questions.question: Which of the elderly gopis is described as having a wrinkled face and a forehead marked by many grey hairs?
questions.explanation: Cuda-devi is characterized by her wrinkled facial features and a forehead adorned with numerous grey hairs.

1.222.verse_text: The brahmani Cundari-devi is not as old as the others. Lotus-eyed Lord Krsna glorifies her and treats her with great respect. Gondika-devi shaves the splendid white hairs on her head. Her cheeks are wrinkled with age.

questions.question: Which brahmani is described as being younger than the other elderly associates in Vraja?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Cundari-devi as the brahmani who is not as old as the others.

questions.question: Which personality in Vraja does Lord Krsna glorify and treat with great respect despite her advanced years?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions that the lotus-eyed Lord Krsna glorifies and treats Cundari-devi with great respect.

questions.question: Which Vraja resident is known for shaving the white hairs from her head and possessing wrinkled cheeks?
questions.explanation: The text states that Gondika-devi shaves her splendid white hairs and has cheeks wrinkled with age.

1.223-224.verse_text: Gopi Messengers who Arrange the Meeting of the Divine Couple: Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida-devi and Kantida-devi are the leaders of the gopi messengers who expertly arrange the meeting of Radha and Krsna. These gopis consider Lalita-devi to be their dearmost life and soul. They are counted among the intimate associates of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: Which six gopis are celebrated as the leaders of the messengers who expertly arrange the meetings between Sri Radha and Sri Krsna?
options: Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida-devi, and Kantida-devi
Lalita-devi, Visakha-devi, Citra-devi, Indulekha-devi, Tungavidya-devi, and Campakalata-devi
Rupa-manjari, Rati-manjari, Lavanga-manjari, Rasa-manjari, Kasturi-manjari, and Manjulali-manjari
Dhaniya, Patala, Hamsi, Sarika, Candralekha, and Matali
questions.correct_answer: Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida-devi, and Kantida-devi
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida-devi, and Kantida-devi as the leaders of the gopi messengers responsible for arranging the Divine Couple's meetings.

questions.question: Whom do the gopi messengers Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida-devi, and Kantida-devi consider to be their dearmost life and soul?
questions.explanation: The text states that these six gopi messengers consider Lalita-devi to be their dearmost life and soul.

questions.question: To which group of associates do the gopi messengers headed by Sivada-devi belong?
options: The intimate associates of Lord Krsna
questions.correct_answer: The intimate associates of Lord Krsna
questions.explanation: The text explicitly counts the gopi messengers who arrange the meetings of Radha and Krsna among the intimate associates of Lord Krsna.

1.225.verse_text: At a certain time Srimati Radharani quarrels with Lord Krsna and refuses to see Him. Understanding the hints of Lalita-devi, these gopis then approach Lord Krsna.

questions.question: Which specific gopi serves as the source of hints for the gopi messengers when Srimati Radharani refuses to see Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions that the gopi messengers understand the hints of Lalita-devi to proceed during the quarrel between Radha and Krsna.

questions.question: What is the primary action taken by the gopi messengers, including Sivada-devi and Saumyadarsana-devi, when Srimati Radharani is in a state of quarrel with Lord Krsna?
options: They approach Lord Krsna for mediation
They hide the flute of Lord Krsna
They perform a dance to pacify the Divine Couple
They decorate the forest groves for a reunion
questions.correct_answer: They approach Lord Krsna for mediation
questions.explanation: The text states that upon understanding the hints of Lalita-devi, the gopi messengers approach Lord Krsna to resolve the conflict.

1.226-227.verse_text: These gopis appease Lord Krsna and please Him in different ways. With great effort they convince Him that His actual desire is to meet Radha again. When These gopis bring to Her the gift that Lord Krsna gave them as a peace offering, Srimati Radharani becomes very pleased with them and grants them Her mercy.

1.228-229.verse_text: Sivada-devi was born in the Ragyhu dynasty, Saumyadarsana-devi in the dynasty of the moon-god, Suprasada-devi in the Puru dynasty, Sadasanta-devi in a family of ascetics and Santida and Kantida in brahmana families. By the mercy of Narada Muni they were all able to reside in Vraja.

questions.question: In which dynasty was the gopi Sivada-devi born?
questions.explanation: Sivada-devi is explicitly stated to have been born in the Ragyhu dynasty.

questions.question: From which dynasty did Saumyadarsana-devi originate?
questions.explanation: Saumyadarsana-devi was born in the dynasty of the moon-god.

questions.question: In which dynasty was Suprasada-devi born?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Suprasada-devi as being born in the Puru dynasty.

questions.question: Sadasanta-devi was born into a family associated with which category?
questions.explanation: Sadasanta-devi is described as being born in a family of ascetics.

questions.question: By whose mercy were Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida, and Kantida able to reside in Vraja?
questions.explanation: The reside in Vraja was granted by the mercy of Narada Muni.

questions.question: To which social class do the families of Santida and Kantida belong?
questions.explanation: Santida and Kantida are identified as being born in brahmana families.

1.230.verse_text: The Second Gruop of Gopis: After this first group of gopis there is a second group of gopis, whose love for the divine couple is a little less than that of the first group. This second group of gopis may again be divided into two groups : sama-prema and asama-prema. These gopis will be described in the following verses.

questions.question: What is the primary factor distinguishing the second group of gopis from the first group of gopis in their relationship with the Divine Couple?
options: Their love for the divine couple is slightly less intense
Their service is performed exclusively in the morning
Their residence is situated further from the forest of Vrindavana
Their participation is limited to the spring season
questions.correct_answer: Their love for the divine couple is slightly less intense
questions.explanation: The text states that the second group of gopis possesses a love for the divine couple that is a little less than that of the first group.

questions.question: Into which two categories is the second group of gopis divided based on their quality of love?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly categorizes the second group of gopis into sama-prema and asama-prema.

1.231.verse_text: The sama-prema gopis may be divided into two groups - those who are eternally perfect and those who have attained perfection by engaging in devotional service.

questions.question: What are the two primary categories into which the sama-prema gopis are divided?
options: Those who are eternally perfect and those who have attained perfection through devotional service
Those who reside in Vraja and those who reside in Mathura
Those who are expert in dancing and those who are expert in music
Those who are of the rank of Lalita and those who are of the rank of Visakha
questions.correct_answer: Those who are eternally perfect and those who have attained perfection through devotional service
questions.explanation: The sama-prema gopis are categorized based on whether their perfection is eternal or attained through sadhana-bhakti.

1.232.verse_text: Out of one hundred million gopis, eight hundred thousand are eternally perfect.

questions.question: Out of the total population of one hundred million gopis in Vraja, how many are considered to be eternally perfect (nitya-siddha)?
questions.explanation: It is stated that out of one hundred million gopis, eight hundred thousand are eternally perfect.

1.233-234.verse_text: The direct followers of the eight principal gopis are counted in different ways. Some say they number five thousand, others say they number four or five thousand, still others say they number three or four thousand and still others say they number one thousand.

questions.question: What is the primary range of the number of direct followers of the eight principal gopis, according to the opinion that they number five thousand?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that some consider the number of direct followers to be five thousand.

questions.question: Which of the following is cited as a possible count for the direct followers of the eight principal gopis, spanning from four to five thousand?
questions.explanation: The text notes that some authorities suggest the number of direct followers is four or five thousand.

questions.question: What is the count of direct followers of the eight principal gopis according to the perspective that suggests a range of three to four thousand?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that some individuals calculate the followers of the eight principal gopis as numbering three or four thousand.

questions.question: According to one of the calculations mentioned, what is the fixed number of direct followers attributed to the eight principal gopis?
questions.explanation: The text records a perspective that the direct followers of the eight principal gopis number one thousand.

1.235.verse_text: Some say the followers of the eight principal gopis are divided into many groups and others say they are not divided into groups at all. Some say they are divided into sixteen groups according to the nature of their love for the divine couple.

questions.question: Regarding the classification of the followers of the eight principal gopis, what is the number of groups cited by some, which is determined by the nature of their love for the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The text states that some say the followers are divided into sixteen groups according to the nature of their love for the divine couple.

1.236.verse_text: Some say these gopis are divided into twenty groups, some say there are twenty-five groups, some say there are thirty, some say there are sixty and some say there are sixty-four groups of gopis.

questions.question: Regarding the classification of the gopis into groups, what is the specific number of groups sometimes cited as twenty?
questions.explanation: It is stated that some sources describe the gopis as being divided into exactly twenty groups.

questions.question: Beyond the suggestion of twenty groups, which of the following is explicitly mentioned as a possible number of gopi groups?
questions.explanation: The text lists twenty-five as one of the varying counts for the grouping of the gopis.

questions.question: Which of these counts is specifically mentioned in the discussion regarding the varying classifications of the gopis?
questions.explanation: Thirty is explicitly cited as one of the counts proposed by those discussing the division of the gopi groups.

questions.question: In the variety of perspectives regarding the grouping of the gopis, which of the following numbers is identified?
questions.explanation: The text acknowledges sixty as a possible number for the divisions of the gopis.

questions.question: Among the various traditional views on the categorization of the gopis, what is the highest number mentioned?
questions.explanation: Sixty-four is the highest number of groups mentioned in the listing of different perspectives regarding gopi divisions.

1.237.verse_text: Some say this group of gopis is in turn divided into two subgroups. Others say it is divided into two or three subgroups and others say it is divided into three or four subgroups.

1.238.verse_text: The entire gopi community may be divided in different ways. Some say there are forty groups of gopis and others that there are five hundred groups.

questions.question: How many groups do some say the gopi community is divided into according to the specific count of forty?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that some authorities state there are forty groups of gopis.

questions.question: What is the alternative number of gopi groups mentioned alongside the count of forty?
questions.explanation: The text provides two specific perspectives on the total number of gopi groups, being either forty or five hundred.

1.239-246.verse_text: Aside from the eight varistha-gopas and the eight vara-gopis, sixty four gopis are considered most important. Their names are : 1. Ratnaprabha, 2. Ratikala, 3. Subhadra, 4. Ratika, 5. Sumukhi, 6. Dhanistha, 7. Kalahamsi, 8. Kalapini, 9. Madhavi, 10. Malati, 11. Candrarekha, 12. Kunjari, 13. Harini, 14. Capala, 15. Damni, 16. Surabhi, 17. Subhanana, 18. Kurangaksi, 19. Sucarita, 20. Mandali, 21. Manikundala, 22. Candrika, 23. Candralatika, 24. Pankajaksi, 25. Sumandira, 26. Rasalika, 27. Tilakini, 28. Sauraseni, 29. Sugandhika, 30. Ramani, 31. Kamanagari, 32. Nagari, 33. Nagavenika, 34. Manjumedha, 35. Sumadhura, 36. Sumadhya, 37. Madhureksana, 38. Tanumadhya, 39. Madhuspanda, 40. Gunacuda, 41. Varangada, 42. Tungabhadra, 43. Rasottunga, 44. Rangavati, 45. Susangata, 46. Citrarekha, 47. Vicitrangi, 48. Modini, 49. Madanalasa, 50. Kalakanthi, 51. Sasikala, 52. Kamala, 53. Madhurendira, 54. Kandarpa-sundari, 55. Kamalatika, 56. Prema-manjari, 57. Kaveri, 58. Carukavara, 59. Sukesi, 60. Manjukesi, 61. Harahira, 62. Mahahira, 63. Harakanthi, 64. Manohara.

questions.question: Apart from the eight varistha-gopas and the eight vara-gopis, how many gopis are specifically categorized as the most important?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that sixty-four gopis are considered most important, excluding the eight varistha-gopas and the eight vara-gopis.

questions.question: Which of the following gopis is listed among the sixty-four most important gopis in Vraja?
questions.explanation: Ratnaprabha is the first gopi mentioned in the list of the sixty-four most important gopis.

questions.question: Which gopi, known for her significance, is recorded as the fifty-sixth in the list of sixty-four most important gopis?
questions.explanation: Prema-manjari is identified as the fifty-sixth gopi in the provided catalog of the sixty-four most important gopis.

questions.question: Which of these associates is counted as the sixty-fourth among the most important gopis?
questions.explanation: The text concludes the list of sixty-four gopis with Manohara as the sixty-fourth name.

questions.question: Which gopi is listed as the sixth in the enumeration of the sixty-four most important gopis?
questions.explanation: Dhanistha is explicitly named as the sixth gopi in the sequence of sixty-four important gopis.

1.247.verse_text: The Sammohana-tantra's Description of Srimati Radharani's Eight Principal Friends: "Lilavati, Sadhika, Candrika, Madhavi, Lalita, Vijaya, Gauri and Nandi are the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani."

questions.question: According to the Sammohana-tantra, which of the following is counted among the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The Sammohana-tantra specifically identifies Lilavati as one of the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: Which of these associates is explicitly listed as one of Srimati Radharani's eight principal friends in the Sammohana-tantra?
questions.explanation: Sadhika is listed in the Sammohana-tantra as one of the eight principal friends of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: Which gopi friend of Srimati Radharani, named in the Sammohana-tantra, is also famous for being one of the most prominent gopis?
questions.explanation: Candrika is identified in the Sammohana-tantra text as being one of the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: Which associate is mentioned alongside Lilavati and Sadhika as one of the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani according to the Sammohana-tantra?
questions.explanation: Vijaya is explicitly included in the list of the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani found in the Sammohana-tantra.

questions.question: According to the Sammohana-tantra, which of these is one of the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Nandi is identified as one of the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani in the Sammohana-tantra description.

1.248.verse_text: Another Description of the Eight Principal Gopis: Another quotation from Vedic literature gives the following description : "Kalavati, Srimati, Sudhamukhi, Visakha, Kaumudi, Madhvi and Sarada are the eight prinicpal gopis."

questions.question: Which of the following is counted among the eight principal gopis in the secondary list found in the Vedic literature?
questions.explanation: Kalavati is explicitly mentioned as one of the eight principal gopis in the list provided in the text.

questions.question: Who is included as one of the eight principal gopis alongside Sarada, Kaumudi, and Visakha?
questions.explanation: Srimati is identified as one of the eight principal gopis in this specific Vedic quotation.

questions.question: Which gopi is listed in the Vedic quotation as belonging to the group of eight principal gopis, alongside Madhvi and Sudhamukhi?
questions.explanation: Kaumudi is explicitly named as one of the eight principal gopis in the Vedic account provided.

questions.question: Which of the following gopis is mentioned as one of the eight principal associates in the Vedic description containing Sudhamukhi and Sarada?
questions.explanation: Madhvi is one of the eight principal gopis enumerated in the Vedic quotation described in the text.

questions.question: Which of these gopis is identified alongside Kalavati and Visakha as a principal associate in the Vedic quotation?
questions.explanation: Sudhamukhi is listed as one of the eight principal gopis in the Vedic quotation regarding the divine associates.

1.249-250.verse_text: The numbers presented in this description of the gopis has no actual relevance to the number of eternally liberated gopis in the spiritual world. There the associates of the king and queen of Vrndavana are unlimited in number. No one can count them. We have simply given some hints so the reader may understand the vast number of the associates of the divine couple.

1.251.verse_text: Preparing the divine couple's couch, food, drink and betel nuts, swinging Them on a swing and decorating Them with tilaka are among the many services performed by the gopis. The learned devotees may research this and enumerate all these services.

questions.question: Which specific item of personal adornment are the gopis described as applying to the divine couple?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies decorating the divine couple with tilaka as one of the many services performed by the gopis.

questions.question: Besides preparing the couch, food, and betel nuts, which specific playful activity for the divine couple is mentioned as a service performed by the gopis?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions the swinging of the divine couple on a swing as a service performed by the gopis.

1.252.verse_text: Rupa Goswami prays that the effulgent Krsna-sun may remove the blindness from his eyes and enable him to properly see and understand the various rasa-sastras that describe Sri Sri Radha-Krsna and Their associates.

1.253.verse_text: In the year 1472 of the Saka era (1550 A.D.) on Sunday, the sixth day of the month of Sravana, at Nandagrama, the home of the king of Vraja, this book, the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika was completed.

questions.question: In what specific year of the Saka era was the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika completed?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika was completed in the year 1472 of the Saka era.

questions.question: On which specific day of the week was the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika finalized?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sunday as the day of the week when the compilation was completed.

questions.question: During which lunar month of the Indian calendar was the composition of the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika finished?
questions.explanation: The text mentions the sixth day of the month of Sravana as the date of completion.

questions.question: At which holy place of Vraja was the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika brought to completion?
questions.explanation: The author specifies Nandagrama, the home of the king of Vraja, as the location where the book was completed.

2.1.verse_text: Sri Krsna's Transcendental Form and Attributes: Sri Krsna's form glistens with the black mascara of the sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness. His complexion is the color of a blue lotus flower or a sapphire.

questions.question: What specifically is compared to the black mascara of Sri Krsna’s transcendental handsomeness?
options: The light of His transcendental form
The fragrance of His lotus feet
questions.explanation: The text states that Sri Krsna's form glistens with the black mascara of the sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness.

questions.question: Which two items are used to describe the specific complexion of Sri Krsna?
options: A dark monsoon cloud and a tamala tree
A blue lotus flower and a sapphire
An emerald and a dark night sky
A mountain peak and deep ocean water
questions.correct_answer: A blue lotus flower and a sapphire
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines Sri Krsna's complexion as being the color of a blue lotus flower or a sapphire.

2.2.verse_text: His complexion is as enchanting as an emerald, a tamala tree, or a group of beautiful dark clouds. He is an ocean of nectarean handsomeness.

questions.question: To which precious stone is the complexion of Sri Krsna compared in His enchanting form?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly compares the complexion of Sri Krsna to the color of an emerald.

questions.question: Which specific type of tree is used to describe the beautiful dark complexion of Sri Krsna?
questions.explanation: Sri Krsna's complexion is described as being as enchanting as a tamala tree.

questions.question: The complexion of Sri Krsna is compared to a gathering of which natural phenomenon in the sky?
questions.explanation: The text describes Sri Krsna's complexion as being as enchanting as a group of beautiful dark clouds.

2.3.verse_text: He wears yellow garments and a garland of forest flowers. He is decorated with various jewels and He is a great reservoir of the nectar of many transcendental pastimes.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna is described as wearing garments that are yellow.

questions.question: What type of garland adorns Lord Krsna according to His description as a reservoir of pastimes?
options: A garland of gold and pearls
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna wears a garland made of forest flowers.

questions.question: Besides His garments and flower garland, with what else is Lord Krsna decorated?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna is explicitly described as being decorated with various jewels.

2.4.verse_text: He has long, curling hair and He is anointed with many fragrant scents. His handsome crown is decorated with many different flowers.

questions.question: What is the specific description of the hair style of Sri Krishna?
options: Flowing straight down to the waist
questions.explanation: Sri Krishna is described as having long, curling hair.

questions.question: With what substance is the transcendental body of Sri Krishna anointed?
questions.explanation: Sri Krishna is anointed with many fragrant scents.

questions.question: What items decorate the handsome crown worn by Sri Krishna?
questions.explanation: The handsome crown of Sri Krishna is decorated with many different flowers.

2.5.verse_text: His handsome forehead is splendidly decorated with tilaka markings and curling locks of hair. The playful movements of His raised, dark eyebrows enchant the hearts of the gopis.

questions.question: What specifically adorns the handsome forehead of Sri Krsna?
options: Tilaka markings and curling locks of hair
A crown of peacock feathers and jewels
Lotus petals and dabs of sandalwood
questions.correct_answer: Tilaka markings and curling locks of hair
questions.explanation: Sri Krsna's forehead is described as being decorated with tilaka and locks of hair that curl.

questions.question: What effect do the playful movements of Sri Krsna's raised, dark eyebrows have upon the gopis?
options: They cause the gopis to hide in shame
They enchant the hearts of the gopis
They make the gopis dance with joy
They fill the gopis with great fear
questions.correct_answer: They enchant the hearts of the gopis
questions.explanation: The playful, dark, raised eyebrows of Sri Krsna are described as enchanting the hearts of the gopis.

2.6.verse_text: His rolling eyes are as splendid as red and blue lotus flowers. The tip of His nose is as handsome as the beak of Garuda, the king of birds.

questions.question: Which two types of flowers are compared to the splendor of Lord Krsna's rolling eyes?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna's rolling eyes are specifically described as being as splendid as red and blue lotus flowers.

questions.question: To which avian feature is the tip of Lord Krsna's nose compared in terms of its handsomeness?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the tip of Lord Krsna's nose is as handsome as the beak of Garuda, the king of birds.

2.7.verse_text: His charming ears and cheeks are decorated with earrings made of various jewels.

questions.question: What specifically decorates the charming ears and cheeks of Lord Krsna?
options: Strings of brilliant pearls and sapphires
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's ears and cheeks are adorned with earrings crafted from various precious jewels.

2.8.verse_text: His handsome lotus face is as splendid as millions of moons. He speaks many charming jokes and His chin is exquisitely handsome.

questions.question: What is the splendor of the handsome lotus face of the gopa known as Arjuna?
options: As bright as a burning fire
As splendid as millions of moons
As cool as a thousand autumn suns
As radiant as a polished sapphire
questions.correct_answer: As splendid as millions of moons
questions.explanation: The text describes the handsome lotus face of the gopa Arjuna as being as splendid as millions of moons.

questions.question: What is the nature of the speech characterizing the gopa Arjuna?
options: He sings verses of the Vedas
He shouts loudly at the calves
questions.explanation: It is stated that the gopa Arjuna speaks many charming jokes.

questions.question: How is the chin of the gopa Arjuna described?
questions.explanation: The gopa Arjuna is noted for having an exquisitely handsome chin.

2.9.verse_text: His handsome, smooth and charming neck bends in three places. Decorated with a necklace of pearls, the beauty of His neck enchants the residents of the three planetary systems.

questions.question: Which specific part of Lord Krsna's body is described as being handsome, smooth, and charming while bending in three places?
questions.explanation: The text specifically describes Lord Krsna's neck as handsome, smooth, charming, and bending in three places.

questions.question: With which type of ornament is the neck of Lord Krsna adorned?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna's neck is explicitly described as being decorated with a necklace of pearls.

questions.question: The beauty of Lord Krsna's neck is said to enchant the residents of which realm?
options: The residents of the three planetary systems
The inhabitants of the spiritual world
The denizens of the heavenly planets
questions.correct_answer: The residents of the three planetary systems
questions.explanation: The text states that the beauty of Lord Krsna's neck enchants the residents of the three planetary systems.

2.10.verse_text: Decorated with a necklace of pearls and with the Kaustubha gem, which shines like lightning, Krsna's handsome chest longs to enjoy the company of the beautiful gopis.

questions.question: Which specific ornament on Krsna's chest is described as shining with the brilliance of lightning?
questions.explanation: Krsna's chest is adorned with the Kaustubha gem, which is explicitly noted for its lightning-like radiance.

questions.question: Besides the Kaustubha gem, what type of necklace is worn upon Krsna's chest?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Krsna's chest as being decorated with a necklace of pearls alongside the Kaustubha gem.

questions.question: Whose company does Krsna long for as His chest is adorned with the Kaustubha gem?
questions.explanation: Krsna's handsome chest is described as longing to enjoy the company of the beautiful gopis.

2.11.verse_text: Decorated with bracelets and armlets, Krsna's arms hang down to His knees. His reddish lotus hands are decorated with various auspicious signs.

questions.question: Which specific bodily ornaments adorn the arms of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's arms are decorated with bracelets and armlets.

questions.question: To what length do the arms of Lord Krsna extend when they hang down?
options: To the middle of His thighs
questions.explanation: It is mentioned that Krsna's arms possess the lakshana of hanging down to His knees.

questions.question: What is the color of Lord Krsna's lotus-like hands?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Krsna's lotus hands are reddish in color.

2.12.verse_text: Krsna's hands are beautifully decorated with the auspicious signs of a club, conchshell, barleycorn, parasol, half-moon, rod for controlling elephants, flag, lotus flower, sacrificial post, plow, pitcher and fish.

questions.question: Which auspicious mark resembling a tool for controlling elephants is present on the hands of Krsna?
questions.explanation: Krsna's hands are explicitly described as bearing the sign of the rod for controlling elephants among other auspicious marks.

questions.question: Besides the club, conchshell, and barleycorn, which celestial object is listed as one of the auspicious signs on Krsna's palms?
questions.explanation: The verse lists the parasol as one of the twelve auspicious signs decorating Krsna's hands.

questions.question: Which of these agricultural items is listed as an auspicious sign found on the hands of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The plow is specifically mentioned as one of the signs marked upon Krsna's hands.

questions.question: Among the auspicious marks on Krsna's hands, which item is used to contain sacred water or offerings?
questions.explanation: The pitcher is clearly identified as one of the twelve auspicious signs on Krsna's hands.

questions.question: Which aquatic creature serves as an auspicious sign upon the palms of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The fish is the final sign listed in the description of the auspicious marks on Krsna's hands.

questions.question: Which of these religious implements is included in the list of auspicious signs marked on Krsna's hands?
questions.explanation: The sacrificial post is listed among the divine signs that decorate the hands of Krsna.

2.13.verse_text: Krsna's charming abdomen is the pastime abode of handsomeness. His nectarean back seems to long for the playful touch of the beautiful gopis.

questions.question: What quality is specifically attributed to Krsna's abdomen as being its pastime abode?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Krsna's abdomen as the pastime abode of handsomeness.

questions.question: Whose playful touch is Krsna's nectarean back said to long for?
questions.explanation: The description states that Krsna's nectarean back seems to long for the playful touch of the beautiful gopis.

2.14.verse_text: The nectarean lotus flower that is Lord Krsna's hips bewilders the demigod Cupid. Krsna's thighs are like two beautiful plantain trees that charm the hearts of all women.

questions.question: Which part of Lord Krsna's transcendental body is compared to a nectarean lotus flower that bewilder the demigod Cupid?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna's hips are described as a nectarean lotus flower that enchants Cupid.

questions.question: To which objects are the beautiful thighs of Lord Krsna compared?
questions.explanation: Krsna's thighs are explicitly described as two beautiful plantain trees that charm the hearts of all women.

questions.question: Whose hearts are charmed by the sight of the beautiful, plantain-like thighs of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text states that Krsna's thighs charm the hearts of all women.

2.15.verse_text: Krsna's knees are very splendid, charming and handsome. His charming lotus feet are decorated with jeweled ankle-bells.

questions.question: With what specific items are the charming lotus feet of Lord Krsna decorated?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lord Krsna's charming lotus feet are decorated with jeweled ankle-bells.

questions.question: How are the knees of Lord Krsna described in terms of their physical appearance?
options: As very splendid, charming and handsome
questions.correct_answer: As very splendid, charming and handsome
questions.explanation: The text characterizes Krsna's knees as very splendid, charming and handsome.

2.16-17.verse_text: Krsna's feet have the luster of roses, and they are decorated with various auspicious markings, such as the markings of the disc, half-moon, octagon, triangle, barleycorn, sky, parasol, waterpot, conchshell, cow's hoofprint, svastika, rod for controlling elephants, lotus flower, bow and jambu fruit.

questions.question: Which specific floral color is associated with the luster of Krsna's lotus feet?
questions.explanation: Krsna's feet are explicitly described as having the luster of roses.

questions.question: Which of the following is among the auspicious markings found on the lotus feet of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text lists the octagon as one of the specific auspicious markings adorning Krsna's feet.

questions.question: Which of these markings is described as being present on Krsna's lotus feet?
questions.explanation: The barleycorn is included in the catalog of auspicious signs on Krsna's feet.

questions.question: Which instrument for controlling elephants is counted as an auspicious marking on Krsna's lotus feet?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies a rod for controlling elephants as a marking on Krsna's feet.

questions.question: Which fruit is mentioned as a marking on the lotus feet of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The jambu fruit is listed as one of the auspicious markings on Krsna's lotus feet.

2.18.verse_text: Krsna's handsome lotus feet are like two oceans filled with the happiness of pure love. His reddish toes are decorated with the row fo full moons that are His toenails.

questions.question: What analogy is used to describe the nature of Krsna's handsome lotus feet in terms of the happiness they contain?
options: Two oceans filled with the happiness of pure love
Two blooming lilies floating in the Yamuna
Two reservoirs of nectar meant for the gopis
Two brilliant suns shining in the sky of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: Two oceans filled with the happiness of pure love
questions.explanation: Krsna's lotus feet are explicitly described as being like two oceans filled with the happiness of pure love.

questions.question: How are the toenails of Krsna's reddish toes metaphorically described?
options: As a row of full moons
questions.correct_answer: As a row of full moons
questions.explanation: Krsna's reddish toes are decorated with the row of full moons that are His toenails.

2.19.verse_text: Although we have sometimes compared Krsna's handsomeness to various things, nothing can actually be equal to it. In this place we have given a small indication of Krsna's handsomeness to arouse the attraction of the reader.

2.20.verse_text: Krsna's Friends: Now Lord Krsna's friends will be described. The most important of Krsna's friends is His elder brother, Balarama.

questions.question: Who is recognized as the most prominent among the friends of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Balarama as the most important of Krsna's friends.

questions.question: What is the familial relationship between Lord Krsna and Balarama regarding their age?
questions.explanation: The text states that Balarama holds the position of being Krsna's elder brother.

2.21.verse_text: Different Kinds of Friends: Krsna's friends are divided into four groups : 1. well-wishing friends (suhrt), 2. ordinary friends (sakha), 3. more confidential friends (priya-sakha), and 4. initmate friends (priya-narma-sakha)

questions.question: Into how many distinct categories are the friends of Lord Krsna classified according to their relationship with Him?
questions.explanation: Lord Krsna's friends are categorized into four groups: suhrt, sakha, priya-sakha, and priya-narma-sakha.

questions.question: What is the specific Sanskrit designation for the group of Krsna's friends known as well-wishing friends?
questions.explanation: The well-wishing friends are designated as suhrt.

questions.question: Which of the four groups of Krsna's friends is referred to as the intimate friends?
questions.explanation: The intimate friends of Krsna are known as the priya-narma-sakha.

questions.question: How are the confidential friends of Krsna identified in the fourfold classification of His associates?
questions.explanation: The more confidential friends are identified as the priya-sakha.

2.22.verse_text: Well-wishing Friends (suhrt): The well-wishing friends include Krsna's cousins : Subhadra, Kundala, Dandi and Mandala. Sunandana, Nandi, Anandi and others who accompany Krsna as He herds the cows and calves in Vrndavana forest are also well-wishing friends.

questions.question: Which specific individuals are identified as Krsna's cousins among the suhrt-sakhas?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists Subhadra, Kundala, Dandi, and Mandala as the cousins among the suhrt-sakhas.

questions.question: Which companions accompany Krsna as He herds the cows and calves in the Vrndavana forest, categorizing them as suhrt-sakhas?
questions.explanation: Sunandana, Nandi, Anandi, and others are identified as the suhrt-sakhas who accompany Krsna while herding cows and calves.

2.23-24.verse_text: Also included among the well-wishing friends are Subhadra, Mandalibhadra, Bhadravardhana, Gobhata, Yaksendra, Bhata, Bhadranga, Virabhadra, Mahaguna, Kulavira, Mahabhima, Divyasakti, Suraprabha, Ranasthira and others. These well-wishing friends are older than Krsna and they try to protect Him from any danger.

questions.question: Which among the following well-wishing friends of Krsna is explicitly mentioned as belonging to the group that protects Him from danger?
questions.explanation: Subhadra is listed among the well-wishing friends who are older than Krsna and strive to protect Him.

questions.question: Besides Subhadra, which of the following is also recognized as an older well-wishing friend tasked with Krsna's protection?
questions.explanation: Mandalibhadra is identified as one of the older well-wishing friends responsible for protecting Krsna.

questions.question: What is the relative age of the well-wishing friends like Bhadravardhana and Yaksendra in comparison to Krsna?
options: They are the same age as Krsna
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that these well-wishing friends, including Bhadravardhana and Yaksendra, are older than Krsna.

questions.question: Which of these associates is counted among the well-wishing friends who watch over Krsna's safety?
questions.explanation: Virabhadra is listed as one of the protectors among the well-wishing friends.

questions.question: Which of the following gopas is included in the list of well-wishing friends who are older than Krsna?
questions.explanation: Ranasthira is explicitly named as a well-wishing friend in the provided group.

2.25.verse_text: Krsna's parents love their son very dearly. They consider Him many millions of times more important than their own life's breath. Very frightened that the demon Kamsa would harm their son, they engaged these well-wishing friends (suhrt) to protect Him. The leader of these well-wishing friends is a boy named Vijayaksa, whose mother, Ambika-devi, was Krsna's nurse. Ambika-devi worshiped goddess Parvati and performed great austerities to get a powerful son who could protect Krsna.

questions.question: Who is the leader of the well-wishing friends (suhrt) engaged by Nanda and Yasoda to protect Krsna from the demon Kamsa?
questions.explanation: Vijayaksa is identified as the leader of the well-wishing friends (suhrt) whose purpose is to guard Krsna.

questions.question: Who is the mother of the cowherd boy Vijayaksa, the leader of Krsna's protectors?
questions.explanation: Ambika-devi is explicitly named as the mother of Vijayaksa.

questions.question: Which specific service did Ambika-devi perform in relation to Krsna?
options: She served as Krsna's music teacher
She served as Krsna's garland maker
questions.explanation: The text states that Ambika-devi was Krsna's nurse.

questions.question: To which deity did Ambika-devi perform great austerities to attain a powerful son capable of protecting Krsna?
questions.explanation: Ambika-devi performed great austerities specifically worshiping goddess Parvati to obtain a powerful son.

2.26.verse_text: Subhadra: Subhadra has a splendid dark complexion. He wears yellow garments and various ornaments.

questions.question: What is the complexion of the cowherd boy Subhadra?
questions.explanation: Subhadra is explicitly described as having a splendid dark complexion.

questions.question: What is the color of the garments worn by Subhadra?
questions.explanation: Subhadra wears garments that are yellow in color.

2.27.verse_text: Subhadra's father is Upananda, and his mother the chaste and faithful Tula-devi. Kundalata-devi will become his wife. Subhadra is full of the glory of youth.

questions.question: Who is the father of the gopa Subhadra?
questions.explanation: Subhadra's father is identified as Upananda.

questions.question: Who is the mother of the cowherd Subhadra?
questions.explanation: Subhadra's mother is the chaste and faithful Tula-devi.

questions.question: Who is the wife of Subhadra?
questions.explanation: Kundalata-devi is the wife of Subhadra.

2.28-29.verse_text: Krsna's Ordinary Friends (sakha): Visala, Vrsabha, Ojasvi, Devaprastha, Varuthapa, Mandara, Kusumapida, Manibandhakara, Mandara, Candana, Kunda, Kalindi, Kulika and many others are included in the group of Krsna's ordinary friends (sakha).
